Input

Stable

Form input component with labels, validation states, icons, and full accessibility support.

Overview

The Input component provides a feature-rich form input with built-in label support, error states, helper text, optional icon slots, and comprehensive ARIA attributes for accessibility.

Live Demo

Try the Input component in action — including validation, icons, and sizes.

Basic Input

With Icons

Sizes

Validation

We'll never share your email.

Basic Usage

A simple text input with a label and placeholder.

input-basic.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputBasic() {
  return (
    <Input
      label="Email"
      type="email"
      placeholder="you@example.com"
    />
  );
}

Sizes

Three sizes accommodate different layout densities.

input-sizes.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputSizes() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-col gap-4">
      <Input size="sm" placeholder="Small" />
      <Input size="md" placeholder="Medium" />
      <Input size="lg" placeholder="Large" />
    </div>
  );
}

States

Error State

Use the error prop to show validation feedback. Error messages are announced by screen readers via role="alert".

Please enter a valid email address.

input-error.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputError() {
  return (
    <Input
      label="Email"
      type="email"
      value="invalid"
      error="Please enter a valid email address."
    />
  );
}

Helper Text

Use the helperText prop for contextual guidance below the input.

input-helper.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputHelper() {
  return (
    <Input
      label="Password"
      type="password"
      helperText="Must be at least 8 characters."
    />
  );
}

Disabled State

Disabled inputs show reduced opacity and prevent user interaction.

input-disabled.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputDisabled() {
  return (
    <Input
      label="Username"
      value="johndoe"
      disabled
    />
  );
}

Full Width

Set fullWidth to make the input span its container — essential for responsive forms and search bars.

input-fullwidth.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputFullWidth() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full">
      <Input label="Search" placeholder="Type to search..." fullWidth />
    </div>
  );
}

Icon Slots

Add icons inside the input using leftIcon and rightIcon props.

input-icons.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputIcons() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-col gap-4">
      <Input
        label="Search"
        placeholder="Type to search..."
        leftIcon={<SearchIcon />}
      />
      <Input
        label="Password"
        type="password"
        rightIcon={<EyeIcon />}
      />
    </div>
  );
}

Edge Cases & Error States

Very Long Placeholder Text

Long placeholder text does not wrap — it overflows horizontally with a standard input. Keep placeholders concise (under 40 characters). For longer contextual hints, use helperText.

Both Icons Simultaneously

leftIcon and rightIcon can be used together. The left icon gets pl-10 padding, the right icon gets pr-10 padding. Both use pointer-events-none so clicks pass through to the input.

input-dual-icons.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputDualIcons() {
  return (
    <Input
      label="Search"
      leftIcon={<SearchIcon />}
      rightIcon={<ClearIcon />}
    />
  );
}

Label Auto-ID Collisions

The label's auto-generated id is derived by lowercasing and hyphenating the label text. If you have multiple inputs with the same label on one page (e.g., two form sections both labeled "Email"), provide explicit id props to avoid ID collisions.

input-custom-id.tsx 
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputCustomId() {
  return (
    <>
      <Input label="Email" id="billing-email" />
      <Input label="Email" id="shipping-email" />
    </>
  );
}

Error/Helper Interaction

When both error and helperText are provided, only the error message is shown. Helper text is hidden to avoid confusion. When the error is cleared (set to undefined), the helper text reappears automatically.

input-error-cleared.tsx 
import React from 'react';
import { Input } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function InputWithValidation() {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState('');
  const error = value.length > 0 && value.length < 3
    ? 'Must be at least 3 characters.'
    : undefined;

  return (
    <Input
      label="Username"
      value={value}
      onChange={(e) => setValue(e.target.value)}
      error={error}
      helperText="Choose a unique username."
    />
  );
}

Best Practices & Usage Guidelines

Form Design

  • Always provide a label — placeholder text is not a replacement for labels
  • Use type="email", type="tel", type="url" for mobile-optimized keyboards
  • Show validation errors on blur (when the user leaves the field), not while typing
  • Use helperText for format hints, error for validation feedback
  • Keep forms to a single column for better readability and mobile usability

Validation Timing

For the best UX, validate on blur (not on every keystroke). Show errors inline next to the relevant field, not as a separate error summary. Clear the error when the user starts correcting it — re-validate on the next blur or submit.

Search Inputs

When using Input as a search field:

  • Use leftIcon with a search magnifying glass icon
  • Set type="search" for proper semantics
  • Consider fullWidth for expanded search bars
  • Use debounce (300ms) on onChange for async search

Accessibility

  • Every input must have a label (visible or via aria-label)
  • Error states set aria-invalid="true" on the input element
  • Error and helper messages use aria-describedby linking
  • Error messages use role="alert" for screen reader announcement
  • Icon containers use pointer-events-none to prevent focus interference

Implementation Details

Auto-ID Generation

The component generates an id from the label text by lowercasing and replacing whitespace with hyphens. This auto-generated ID is used for htmlFor on the label and for aria-describedby linking to error and helper text elements.

ARIA Binding

When error is provided, the input gets aria-invalid="true" and aria-describedby pointing to the error message element. When helperText is provided (without error), aria-describedby points to the helper element. If both are provided, error takes priority in description linking.

Dark Mode

Input uses dark mode variants across border colors, background, text, and placeholder. Focus ring colors also dark-adapt: focus:border-blue-500 becomes dark:focus:border-blue-400. Error states use red-300/red-700 borders and red-600/red-400 text for dark mode.

Props

Prop Type Default Description
label string - Label text rendered above the input. Auto-generates an id.
error string - Error message. Shows red border and alert text when set.
helperText string - Helper text shown below the input (hidden when error is set).
size "sm" | "md" | "lg" "md" Input height and text size.
fullWidth boolean false Spans 100% of parent width.
leftIcon React.ReactNode - Icon rendered inside the input on the left side.
rightIcon React.ReactNode - Icon rendered inside the input on the right side.
id string - Custom id. Auto-generated from label if not provided.
disabled boolean false Disables input and reduces opacity.

All standard <input> attributes (type, placeholder, value, onChange, etc.) are also supported.

Accessibility

  • Label is properly associated via htmlFor matching the input id
  • Error messages use aria-describedby linking and role="alert"
  • aria-invalid is set automatically when the error prop is provided
  • Helper text and error are properly differentiated via aria-describedby
  • Disabled state uses cursor-not-allowed and reduced opacity
  • Icon containers use pointer-events-none to prevent input interference
